title: HTTP API Resources, Representations, Errors, and Handler Layers · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent community archive receives a different publisher mix, malformed feed, validator state, tenant, schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers http api resources, representations, errors, and handler layers into a changed consistent feed-reader HTTP API. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of consistent feed-reader HTTP API with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Design tenant, source, subscription, entry, collection, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions rather than tables.
Separate routing, authority, request admission, application command, repository, representation, and observability with narrow interfaces.
Apply safe and idempotent method semantics, success statuses, preconditions, redirects, and retry guidance consistently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Resource Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 5141 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource semantics, layered handlers, problem details, cancellation, and response finality; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Resource Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design tenant, source, subscription, entry, collection, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions rather than tables. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Go Resource Model, keep Feed Go Resource Model connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: State the Feed Go Resource Model preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
A CRUD endpoint for every table is a coherent API. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Feed Go Resource Model.

For commit to the mission model, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof. This does not satisfy Feed Go Resource Model for consistent feed-reader HTTP API.
